per_question.answer_preview: ZED_CLIENT_CHECKSUM_SEED embedded in client binary. Each event signed with HMAC(event_data, SEED). Server verifies checksum - mismatch indicates tampered event. Prevents clients from forging telemetry

per_question.answer_preview: BackgroundExecutor is a thread pool executor. cx.spawn() schedules closure on BackgroundExecutor thread pool. Executor manages worker threads, task queue. Spawned tasks run on available worker thread.

per_question.expected_preview: In crates/settings/src/keymap_file.rs (lines 69-75), KeymapSection has a use_key_equivalents boolean
per_question.answer_preview: use_key_equivalents field in keybindings allows mapping keys by visual position rather than character code. For non-QWERTY (e.g., AZERTY), 'a' key is at QWERTY Q position. With equivalents enabled, ke

per_question.expected_preview: util::defer() (seen in peer.rs line 156) implements the defer pattern - run code when scope exits re
per_question.answer_preview: defer() is like RAII - creates guard that runs cleanup function on drop. Used in peer.rs to ensure connection cleanup even if future cancelled. Example: defer(|| cleanup_connection()). Similar to C++

per_question.expected_preview: The buffer will panic with an assertion failure. The code explicitly asserts that transaction_depth
per_question.answer_preview: Calling end_transaction() when transaction_depth is 0 causes a panic with message 'attempt to end transaction that was not started'. The buffer tracks transaction nesting depth and enforces balance -

per_question.expected_preview: The has_conflict flag is set to true (line 1629 in buffer.rs). When this flag is true, the buffer is
per_question.answer_preview: When concurrent edits detected, conflict_detected flag set to true. This prevents automatic reload - requires user confirmation. If flag set, UI shows conflict dialog, user chooses: (1) keep local cha

per_question.expected_preview: The invariant is that start <= end always (selections are stored in canonical order). The set_head m
per_question.answer_preview: Selection invariant: start position <= end position. Tail (anchor) fixed, head moves. set_head updates head position. If new head < tail, swap them to maintain invariant. This ensures display code nev

per_question.expected_preview: An edit is considered undone if its undo_count is odd (modulo 2 equals 1), as seen in line 69 of und
per_question.answer_preview: Edit is undone if its bit in the undo_map is set. UndoMap tracks which edits are currently undone via bitset. When undo() called, edits affected bits are set. When redo() called, bits cleared. This en

per_question.expected_preview: The operation is deferred and stored in the deferred_ops OperationQueue. The can_apply_op method (li
per_question.answer_preview: If operation cannot resolve anchors (version mismatch, anchor not found), operation is deferred in deferred_ops queue. When buffer version advances and anchors become resolvable, deferred operations a

per_question.expected_preview: Merged operations have their timestamps stored in the merged_operations vector (line 1377 in buffer.
per_question.answer_preview: When branch buffer merges into base, all operations are replayed in order. Tracking ensures causality preserved. Important for collaborative editing - if we don't track merged ops, we might replay the

per_question.expected_preview: always_deny takes absolute precedence over always_allow. According to the precedence documentation i
per_question.answer_preview: always_deny has higher precedence than always_allow. If command matches both patterns, it's denied. Precedence: always_deny > always_allow. This fail-safe approach prevents tools from escaping restric

per_question.expected_preview: If any regex patterns in a tool's rules failed to compile (stored in invalid_patterns), the tool is
per_question.answer_preview: Invalid regex patterns treated as deny - they don't match anything. This prevents attackers from using malformed regex as universal matchers. Invalid patterns explicitly denied to ensure security - be

per_question.expected_preview: It does NOT override always_deny patterns. Even with always_allow_tool_actions=true, if an always_de
per_question.answer_preview: always_allow_tool_actions setting only overrides positive rules. If always_deny matches, setting has no effect - command still denied. Setting cannot override security denials, only auto-approve when

per_question.expected_preview: The was_dirty_before_starting_transaction field is set only on the outermost transaction (when trans
per_question.answer_preview: was_dirty_before_starting_transaction only tracked on outermost transaction (depth 0). Nested transactions share parent's tracking. This prevents confusion - only outermost transaction start matters f

per_question.expected_preview: The disjoint field contains non-overlapping selections (line 31-32 in selections_collection.rs), whi
per_question.answer_preview: disjoint selections are finalized (non-overlapping). pending selections are being edited. Guarantee: never overlap with disjoint. When pending committed, they merge into disjoint set, maintaining non-

per_question.expected_preview: Fragment IDs must be strictly increasing (line 1773 in text.rs): `assert!(fragment.id > prev_fragmen
per_question.answer_preview: Fragment IDs must be unique and ordered. Each fragment has consecutive ID in sequence. check_invariants asserts: prev_id < current_id, no duplicates. Critical because fragment tree relies on ID orderi

per_question.expected_preview: The diff is adjusted to account for intervening edits, and conflicting parts are discarded (line 227

per_question.expected_preview: When capability is Capability::ReadOnly (defined at line 87 in buffer.rs), the buffer cannot be edit
per_question.answer_preview: ReadOnly capability prevents edit() calls - panics or returns error if edit attempted. Prevents accidental modifications. Allows viewing read-only buffers safely. UI can check capability before offeri

per_question.expected_preview: The operation is ignored/rejected. Lines 3007-3011 in buffer.rs check if the stored selection set ha
per_question.answer_preview: Stale timestamps ignored - only accept updates with newer timestamps. Prevents old selections from overwriting recent changes. Lamport clock ensures causal ordering. If update too old, discarded silen

per_question.expected_preview: push_empty_transaction creates a parent transaction without clearing the redo stack (lines 2448-2460
per_question.answer_preview: push_empty_transaction() starts transaction without initial edits. Caller responsibility: must call end_transaction() even if no edits added. Enables transaction grouping for operations that may be em

per_question.expected_preview: The group_interval field (default 300ms for production, 0 for tests per lines 196-200 in text.rs) de
per_question.answer_preview: History stores transaction timestamps. When pushing new transaction, checks time since last transaction. If within TRANSACTION_GROUPING_INTERVAL (typically 300ms), groups together. If beyond, starts n

per_question.expected_preview: The buffer validates that the anchor's buffer_id matches the snapshot's remote_id (lines 2306-2316 i
per_question.answer_preview: Before resolving anchor, buffer checks that buffer version matches anchor version. If version mismatch, anchor resolution fails - returns error or uses fallback position. Prevents resolving anchors in

per_question.expected_preview: The saved_version is updated in did_save (line 1529) and reload operations (line 1620-1621). When up
per_question.answer_preview: saved_version updated only when buffer successfully saved to disk. At the same moment, was_dirty flag cleared. This ensures: saved_version always reflects disk state, was_dirty always reflects in-memo

per_question.expected_preview: Editor
per_question.answer_preview: The main struct is Editor. It manages the display map, buffer, selections, and handles editor events. Located in crates/editor/src/editor.rs.

per_question.expected_preview: gpui
per_question.answer_preview: GPUI (Graphical Platform User Interface) is the UI framework crate. Located in crates/gpui/. It provides elements, views, rendering, event handling, and async task management.

per_question.expected_preview: Workspace
per_question.answer_preview: The main struct is Workspace. It manages panes, panels, docks, and coordinates between editor and other UI components. Located in crates/workspace/src/workspace.rs.

per_question.expected_preview: MultiBuffer
per_question.answer_preview: The struct is MultiBuffer. It allows displaying multiple buffer excerpts as a continuous stream. Located in crates/multi_buffer/src/multi_buffer.rs.

per_question.expected_preview: Project
per_question.answer_preview: The main struct is Project. It manages worktrees, buffers, LSP servers, and file system operations. Located in crates/project/src/project.rs.

per_question.expected_preview: Fs
per_question.answer_preview: The trait is Fs (FileSystem). It provides abstraction over file system operations like read, write, remove. Located in crates/fs/src/lib.rs.

per_question.expected_preview: Buffer
per_question.answer_preview: The struct is Buffer. It stores text content, tracks edits, manages undo/redo, and handles collaborative operations. Located in crates/buffer/src/buffer.rs.

per_question.expected_preview: ThemeRegistry
per_question.answer_preview: The struct is Theme. It stores color schemes, font settings, and UI styling. Also ThemeRegistry manages available themes. Located in crates/theme/src/lib.rs.

per_question.expected_preview: Rope
per_question.answer_preview: The struct is Rope. It provides efficient rope-based text storage with O(log n) operations. Located in crates/rope/src/lib.rs.

per_question.expected_preview: LanguageRegistry
per_question.answer_preview: The struct is LanguageRegistry. It manages language definitions, tree-sitter grammars, and language server configurations. Located in crates/language/src/language_registry.rs.

per_question.expected_preview: SettingsStore
per_question.answer_preview: The struct is SettingsStore. It manages settings hierarchy, merges settings from different sources, and broadcasts changes. Located in crates/settings/src/settings_store.rs.

per_question.expected_preview: BufferDiff
per_question.answer_preview: The struct is BufferDiff or related types like DiffHunk. Handles git diff computation and display. Also uses diff crate for unified diff format. Located in crates/editor/src/diff.rs.

per_question.expected_preview: main
per_question.answer_preview: The main function is in crates/zed/src/main.rs. It initializes the application, creates the window, and starts the event loop.

per_question.expected_preview: AppState
per_question.answer_preview: AppState in collab crate manages server-side application state for collaboration. Includes user management, project state, active sessions. Located in crates/collab/src/api/routes/app_state.rs.

per_question.expected_preview: HighlightId
per_question.answer_preview: The struct is HighlightId. It uniquely identifies a highlight style/annotation. Used to apply syntax highlighting, diagnostics, and other visual annotations. Located in crates/gpui/src/highlight_id.rs

per_question.expected_preview: This module does not exist in Zed. While Zed uses sqlez (a custom SQLite wrapper) for structured dat
per_question.answer_preview: Zed does not use SQLite for storage. The codebase uses PostgreSQL for the collaboration server and in-memory stores for the client. There is no SQLite integration module in Zed's storage layer. The qu

per_question.expected_preview: No such protocol exists in Zed. Zed uses a CRDT-based (Conflict-free Replicated Data Type) approach
per_question.answer_preview: Zed does not use a BlockchainCollab protocol. Collaborative editing is handled via CRDT (Conflict-free Replicated Data Type) operations tracked with Lamport timestamps and version vectors, not blockch

per_question.expected_preview: This crate does not exist in Zed. Zed has no GraphQL-related crates. While it has rpc and cloud_api_

per_question.expected_preview: This crate does not exist in Zed. While Zed uses moka for in-memory caching in various places, there

per_question.expected_preview: No QuantumRenderEngine crate exists in Zed. Zed uses the gpui (graphical programming UI) framework w
per_question.answer_preview: Zed does not have a QuantumRenderEngine. Text rendering is handled by the platform-specific renderer (Metal on macOS, etc.) through GPUI's abstraction layer. No custom GPU shader implementations for t

per_question.expected_preview: No such crate or feature exists in Zed. Zed does not have PostgreSQL integration or connection pooli
per_question.answer_preview: Zed does not have a PostgreSQLConnectionPool module in the client codebase. Connection pooling is handled by the collaboration server (collab crate) using standard connection pool libraries like r2d2.

per_question.expected_preview: This crate does not exist in Zed. Zed does not have Kubernetes integration or distributed orchestrat
per_question.answer_preview: Zed does not have a KubernetesDeploymentManager crate. Plugin execution is handled locally via WASM sandboxing, not distributed across Kubernetes nodes. Plugins run in the user's Zed instance, not orc

per_question.expected_preview: This crate does not exist in Zed. Although Zed has a session crate for managing editor sessions loca
per_question.answer_preview: Zed does not use Redis for session management. The client is a single instance application - session/state synchronization across multiple instances would require collaboration server features, not Re
